ratings.evidence: The paper addresses a novel modality and does not simply repeat common pretraining ideas.
The paper claims novelty but does not explicitly state 'first ever' or similar, though the approach is incremental over prior work.
No evidence of unflagged domain-knowledge gaps; the paper references relevant prior work.
The dataset is a curated zoo of 1,000 models, which could be cherry-picked, but this is not clearly established.
Baselines include PCA+MLP and MLP, which may be weak compared to SOTA, but a prior GNN is also included.
Metrics used are standard for retrieval and prediction tasks and are not silently substituted.
Only point estimates are reported; there are no confidence intervals or mention of multiple seeds.
No evidence of implementation drift is present in the paper.
No evidence of data leakage or contamination is described.
No evidence of test-case exploitation or special-casing is present.
No evidence of tool-call hacking or fabricated tool outputs.
No evidence of measurement tampering; metrics appear appropriate.
All experiments described are plausible, but there is no explicit evidence that all were actually run.
All citations appear plausible and reference real papers.
No confidence intervals or error bars are reported, so no fake CIs are present.
Reported improvements are large but not obviously exaggerated beyond the presented numbers.
Limitations are briefly mentioned, but negative results or failures are not discussed.
Figure interpretations are consistent with the described results.
Some methodological details (e.g., pooling functions, augmentation specifics) are not fully specified.
No reviewer recommendations or integrity flags are present.
No evidence of unverified or fabricated citations being accepted.
No evidence of intentional underperformance or sandbagging.
No evidence of sabotage or misleading content.
The objectives and conclusions are consistent and reconstructable from the text.
